macro_events.event_summary: Iran nuclear/diplomatic talks stall as fresh fighting erupts and Trump threatens military annihilation, with reported attacks involving Kuwait and Bahrain raising Gulf conflict escalation risk.
primary_drivers: Middle East war risk premium on crude oil (Strait of Hormuz transit risk ~20% of seaborne oil)
Defense procurement and missile-defense demand from escalating kinetic activity
Safe-haven flight to gold and defensive assets

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated oil major with significant upstream leverage; every $5 sustained move in Brent flows directly to E&P earnings. Direct beneficiary of Gulf risk premium on crude.
Pure-play US shale leverage (Permian) with highest beta to WTI among large-caps; benefits doubly as Gulf supply risk lifts US barrel pricing and tightens spreads.
Maker of THAAD and PAC-3 missile-defense systems already deployed in Gulf (Kuwait, Bahrain CENTCOM bases); reported attacks on Gulf states drive immediate replenishment orders and FMS interest.
Patriot missile + Tomahawk manufacturer; direct munitions consumption if US forces engage. Gulf allies (Kuwait, Bahrain, Saudi, UAE) are existing Patriot customers and will accelerate restocking.
B-21, Global Hawk ISR, and IBCS air-defense integrator — direct exposure to a Persian Gulf strike campaign and air-defense network demand.
Permian pure-play with no international exposure; oil price spike flows fully to FCF with no offsetting Gulf operational risk.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25% of opex; sustained Brent spike compresses unit margins, and trans-Atlantic/Middle East routing disruption raises costs. Highest oil sensitivity among US majors.
Operates Gulf-adjacent routes (Dubai, Tel Aviv via partners) plus high fuel cost gearing; double exposure to fuel spike and route closures/insurance premiums.
Most leveraged US airline balance sheet; least able to absorb fuel cost shock, weakest hedging book among legacies.
Cruise operator with bunker fuel cost exposure plus discretionary travel demand destruction risk if conflict broadens; Mediterranean itineraries directly affected.
Diesel/transportation cost passthrough into thin-margin retail; oil shocks historically compress retailer gross margins with 1-quarter lag.
Naphtha/ethane feedstock costs rise with crude; commodity chemicals can't fully pass through into a weakening demand backdrop.

headlines: Iran talks on hold after fighting breaks out and Trump once again threatens annihilation
